name: pyappwrite

metadata.name: PyAppWrite
metadata.summary: Third Party Module with Persistent Session

documentation:
Third Party Module with Persistent Session
For more information, please refer to the official [Appwrite Documentation](https://appwrite.io/docs)

classes.name: PyAppWrite.query.Query
documentation: Inherit from dict to allow for easy serialization

methods.name: PyAppWrite.id.ID.__hex_timestamp
documentation: Generate an hex ID based on timestamp

methods.name: PyAppWrite.id.ID.unique
documentation: Generate a unique ID with padding to have a longer ID

methods.name: PyAppWrite.client.Client.set_session
documentation: The user session to authenticate with

methods.name: PyAppWrite.client.Client.set_forwarded_user_agent
documentation: The user agent string of the client that made the request

methods.name: PyAppWrite.services.health.Health.get_certificate
documentation: Get the SSL certificate for a domain

methods.name: PyAppWrite.services.health.Health.get_failed_jobs
documentation: Get number of failed queue jobs

classes.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.any
documentation:
Grants access to anyone.

This includes authenticated and unauthenticated users.

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.user
documentation:
Grants access to a specific user by user ID.

You can optionally pass verified or unverified for
`status` to target specific types of users.

Parameters
----------
id : str
status : str, optional

Returns
-------
str

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.users
documentation:
Grants access to any authenticated or anonymous user.

You can optionally pass verified or unverified for
`status` to target specific types of users.

Parameters
----------
status : str, optional

Returns
-------
str

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.guests
documentation:
Grants access to any guest user without a session.

Authenticated users don't have access to this role.

Returns
-------
str

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.team
documentation:
Grants access to a team by team ID.

You can optionally pass a role for `role` to target
team members with the specified role.

Parameters
----------
id : str
role : str, optional

Returns
-------
str

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.member
documentation:
Grants access to a specific member of a team.

When the member is removed from the team, they will
no longer have access.

Parameters
----------
id : str

Returns
-------
str

methods.name: PyAppWrite.role.Role.label
documentation:
Grants access to a user with the specified label.

Parameters
----------
name : str

Returns
-------
str
Helper class to generate role strings for `Permission`.

methods.name: PyAppWrite.services.users.Users.create_scrypt_modified_user
documentation: Create user with Scrypt modified password
